_comment: COVID mid-crisis — VIX 41→54 on 2020-03-09. Window already contains the Feb 24 spike and subsequent elevated readings, demonstrating window absorption: despite a bigger absolute move than Feb 24, z-score is lower because the window has absorbed the crisis ramp.

expected_band.rationale_pending_trader: SRS CLS-001 ECDF: VIX median=21.055, |deviation|=|54.46-21.055|=33.405. All 20 historical deviations are ≤ 33.405 (window spans 13.68-41.94), so ecdf_rank=20, severity=20/20=1.00. This exemplifies window absorption: VIX absolute move is large (54.46 is peak COVID), but the window already absorbed the Feb 24 spike and the ramp, so z-score only 2.75. Yet ECDF still ranks it 1.00 because 33.4 exceeds every deviation in the window. This is the correct behaviour: within the crisis regime, this is the most extreme reading. Tanh gives 0.14 (understates due to limited z-score within elevated window).
